hero.subtitle: THE FRONTEND PATH · 2026
hero.title: Tutorial apps to real products — that user used.
hero.description: A hands-on, two-course path that teaches modern React deeply all the way to a tested, production-ready full-stack app you build solo, with real accounts, a database and storage powered by a Backend-as-a-Service (Supabase, with Firebase awareness), then shows you how to build software with AI agents the way senior engineers actually work in 2026.

curriculum.heading: THE CURRICULUM
curriculum.title: Two courses + a bonus track. All building on each other.
curriculum.description: Each course follows the same rhythm: understand the concept, watch it built live, build it yourself, then a checkpoint before moving on. The bonus track packages everything for your job hunt.

courseOne.label: Course 01
courseOne.title: Production-Grade React
courseOne.description: Learn React deeply and take it all the way to a tested, shippable full-stack app.
bullets: Testing, Core Web Vitals, a11y & security
courseOne.outcome: You leave able to build & ship a real, full-stack app.

courseTwo.label: Course 02
courseTwo.title: Planned AI Coding
courseTwo.description: Build software with AI agents the way senior engineers do, specify, plan, tasks, implement
courseTwo.outcome: You leave able to ship faster with agents, safely.

bonus.label: 🎁 Bonus Track
bonus.title: Getting Hired
bonus.description: Package your capstone for the real world — CV, portfolio, LinkedIn, interviews. Every module ends with a concrete deliverable reusing the app you already built.
bullets: Know the target — what employers hire for
One-page CV led by the capstone
bonus.outcome: You leave with a job-ready portfolio, CV, and interview prep.

howToLearn.heading: HOW TO LEARN
howToLearn.title: One project carries you the whole way — the way real products actually get built.

step1.title: Learn deep, think product
step1.body: You don't just learn React in isolation. Every concept — hooks, Suspense, performance, Supabase, RSC — is learned through *why it matters to the product*, not just how to call it. By the end of Course 01 you can read a feature brief and think: 'what's the right component shape, the right state model, the right backend call?'

step2.title: Collaborate like a team
step2.body: Real work isn't solo. In Course 02 and the group build you work the way production teams do — taking specs from a PM, translating designs from a Figma file, discussing trade-offs, writing clean pull requests, and reviewing each other's code. The agent workflow is the same one senior engineers use to stay in sync.

step3.title: Ship something real
step3.body: The last step is the one that actually lands you a job or a client: you ship. Deploy the full-stack app live, write the case study, send the first proposal or application — all from the same project you built. The bonus career track just packages what you already did.

capstone.title: One project — from idea to shipped product in your portfolio.
capstone.body: Not toy exercises. You build a real application the way a small team does: PM-driven, design-aware, and production-hardened. That's the kind of work that gets you hired, promoted, or your first paying client.

toolkit.heading: THE TOOLKIT
toolkit.title: The tools you'll actually use.
toolkit.description: The same editor, AI agents, design and shipping stack that senior engineers reach for every day, you'll be comfortable in all of them by the end.

timeline.heading: THE 14-WEEK TIMELINE
timeline.title: Fourteen weeks, one weekend at a time.
timeline.description: Nine weeks to build production React with a real Supabase backend, two to work with AI agents, a two-week group build, then one bonus week to launch your career.
timeline.scheduleInfo: Starts Sat 1 Aug 2026 · 14 weeks · 6 hrs/week · Sat & Sun · 3 hrs/day

rows.title: React Fundamentals
rows.desc: How Rendering Works: JSX, components, props, controlled forms & TypeScript · build a product card

rows.title: Hooks
rows.desc: State & Side Effects: useState, useEffect, custom hooks & first tests · build a live search box

rows.title: Bigger State — Reducers & Context
rows.desc: useReducer, Context & re-render traps · build a cart with a global badge

rows.title: Loading States Done Right — Suspense
rows.desc: Declarative loading & useTransition · build a profile with skeleton loaders

rows.title: Reusable Component Patterns
rows.desc: Compound components & render props · build a Modal/Dialog family

rows.title: Performance & Optimization
rows.desc: Profile first, React 19 Compiler, virtualization · build an infinite feed

rows.title: React Server Components (RSC)
rows.desc: Server vs Client, Server Actions · build a blog post + comment form

rows.title: Backend Without a Backend — Supabase & BaaS
rows.desc: Auth, Postgres, storage & Row-Level Security · give your app real accounts & data

rows.title: Shipping It — Production Essentials
rows.desc: Testing, Core Web Vitals, a11y & security · ship a hardened login form

rows.title: AI On-Ramp + How Agents Work
rows.desc: Prompting, reviewing AI output, context budgets · pick your own project

rows.title: Consistency → Slicing → Spec-Driven Dev
rows.desc: AGENTS.md, vertical slicing, plan mode & CI guardrails · spec a real feature

rows.title: Group Build — Week 1
rows.desc: Drive your own full-stack project feature-by-feature through a full agent workflow

rows.title: Group Build — Week 2 + Demo
rows.desc: Harden, test & ship, then present your finished app on demo day

rows.title: Getting Hired — Career Launch
rows.desc: CV, LinkedIn, live portfolio, freelancing basics & interview prep — package your capstone for the job hunt

details.heading: THE DETAILS
details.title: What you need to know before you join.

schedule.title: Schedule & duration
schedule.desc: 14 weeks · 6 hrs/week — Saturday & Sunday sessions, 3 hours each day, with hands-on labs — 9 weeks of React (including a Supabase backend), 2 of AI coding, and a 2-week group build. Starts August 2026.

format.title: Format
format.desc: Live online classes combined with on-the-job (OJT) mentoring — you code, get reviewed, and improve with direct feedback.

audience.title: Who it's for
audience.desc: Junior & mid developers comfortable with HTML, CSS and JavaScript who want to become production-grade frontend engineers.

instructor.heading: YOUR INSTRUCTOR
instructor.title: Learn from someone who ships & leads.
instructor.name: Nyan Lin Tun
instructor.role: Senior Full-Stack JS Developer & Project Director
instructor.bio: 8+ years building and leading production apps across web, mobile and IoT. I run developer training programs and teach junior devs React, Next.js and agentic coding in a real on-the-job format — this course is that experience, structured.

cta.heading: READY WHEN YOU ARE
cta.title: Let's talk about joining.
cta.description: Seats are limited so I can give real feedback. Send me a message — tell me where you are as a developer and I'll help you figure out if this path is right for you.

footer.copyright: © 2026 Nyan Lin Tun. Production-Grade React & AI Coding — a hands-on path for junior & mid developers.

bonus.heading: BONUS TRACK
bonus.title: Getting Hired — Career Launch Bonus.
bonus.description: Runs right after the build while your capstone is fresh. Every module ends with a concrete deliverable that reuses the app you already shipped — no new project to invent.

modules.title: Preparing to Get Hired
modules.subtitle: Know the target
bullets: Reading job descriptions like a detective
Full-time vs freelance — which path fits you now
modules.deliverable: Write your positioning line: "I build [X] with [tech] for [audience]."

modules.title: CV / Résumé That Gets Read
modules.subtitle: One page that sells you
bullets: One-page dev CV led by the capstone
modules.deliverable: Ship a one-page CV with your capstone as the lead project.

modules.title: LinkedIn Profile Setup
modules.subtitle: Be findable, be credible
modules.deliverable: Publish a LinkedIn profile with headline, About, and your capstone in Projects.

modules.title: Project Portfolio Setup
modules.subtitle: Show, don't tell
bullets: A portfolio site that links everything together
A proper case study with before/after & decisions
modules.deliverable: Live URL + portfolio site + one written case study published.

modules.title: Freelancing & First Clients
modules.subtitle: Getting paid to build
bullets: Where freelance work actually comes from
Using your AI workflow to deliver faster
modules.deliverable: Create one service package page and send your first proposal.

modules.title: Interviews & Landing the Offer
modules.subtitle: Close the deal
bullets: The interview flow — what actually happens
Walking through your capstone like a pro
Common React / JS questions & how to answer them
Negotiation basics — don't leave money on the table
modules.deliverable: Record yourself walking through the capstone in under 3 minutes.

teachingNote.title: Teaching Note — Hands-on from day one.
teachingNote.body: This bonus track reuses the capstone you already built across both courses. No new projects — just packaging, presenting, and leveraging what you've got. It runs right after the build while everything is fresh and deployable.
